title: Use BATCH only for single-partition atomicity
description: Scope a CQL BATCH to one partition for atomicity; never use multi-partition batches as a performance shortcut.
body: Use when several writes to the same partition must apply atomically. Actions: wrap writes that share one partition key in a logged BATCH so they commit together, and for unrelated partitions issue independent asynchronous writes instead. Do not use a multi-partition BATCH to group unrelated inserts; it forces the coordinator to fan out and log every mutation, hurting throughput and creating coordinator pressure. Verify the batch targets a single partition. Validation signals include batches confined to one partition and write latency staying flat under load. Risk boundary: a large multi-partition batch can overload the coordinator and time out, so reserve BATCH for single-partition atomicity and parallelise cross-partition writes from the client.
